---
title: "physiology_environment_association"
format: html
editor: source
---

```{r, message=FALSE}
library(tidyverse)
library(corrplot)
library(lme4)

source("../../R/load_functions.R")
triggerR <- load_trigger_functions()
rm(load_trigger_functions)

dir.create("../../outputs/physiology_environment_association", showWarnings = F)
```

## Get Data

```{r}
trigger_hourly <- if (!exists("trigger_hourly")) {
  triggerR$make_hourly_wide_table(return_meta = T)
} else trigger_hourly
```

## Check correlated variables

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=9}
var_names <- colnames(trigger_hourly$data)
cor_order <- cor(trigger_hourly$data, method = "pearson",
                 use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
hc <- hclust(as.dist(1 - cor_order), method = "complete")
var_names <- var_names[hc$order]




corr_long <- expand_grid(var_y = var_names, var_x = var_names) %>%
  mutate(row_id = match(var_y, var_names),
         col_id = match(var_x, var_names),
         method = case_when(row_id < col_id ~ "spearman",
                            row_id > col_id ~ "pearson",
                            TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(method)) %>%
  mutate(correlation = pmap_dbl(
    .l = list(var_y, var_x, method),
    .f =\(y, x, m) cor(trigger_hourly$data[[y]],
                       trigger_hourly$data[[x]],
                       method = m, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"))) %>%
  mutate(var_x = factor(var_x, levels = var_names),
         var_y = factor(var_y, levels = rev(var_names))) 

rm(var_names, cor_order, hc)
```

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=9}
p_cor <- corr_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = var_x, y = var_y, fill = correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "cornsilk3", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(abs(correlation) >= 0.3, round(correlation, 2), "")),
    size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(limits = c(-1, 1), midpoint = 0, name = "Correlation") +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Correlation matrix",
       subtitle = "Upper triangle: Spearman | Lower triangle: Pearson",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right")
p_cor
```

```{r}
ggsave("../../outputs/physiology_environment_association/correlations.png",
       p_cor, width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 300)
rm(p_cor)
```

#### Check distribution of the variables.

```{r, fig.width=12, fig.height=9}
trigger_hourly$data %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "event") %>%
  pivot_longer(-event, names_to = "measurment", values_to = "value") %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = value)) +
  geom_histogram(fill = "lightblue", color = "darkblue", bins = 40) +
  facet_wrap(~ measurment, scales = "free") +
  theme_bw()
```

```{r}
trigger_hourly$data_log <- trigger_hourly$data %>%
  mutate(across(c(starts_with("pc"), starts_with("pm"), cal, steps, light, uvb), log1p))
```

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=9}
var_names <- colnames(trigger_hourly$data_log)
cor_order <- cor(trigger_hourly$data_log, method = "pearson",
                 use = "pairwise.complete.obs")
hc <- hclust(as.dist(1 - cor_order), method = "complete")
var_names <- var_names[hc$order]




corr_long <- expand_grid(var_y = var_names, var_x = var_names) %>%
  mutate(row_id = match(var_y, var_names),
         col_id = match(var_x, var_names),
         method = case_when(row_id < col_id ~ "spearman",
                            row_id > col_id ~ "pearson",
                            TRUE ~ NA_character_)) %>%
  filter(!is.na(method)) %>%
  mutate(correlation = pmap_dbl(
    .l = list(var_y, var_x, method),
    .f =\(y, x, m) cor(trigger_hourly$data_log[[y]],
                       trigger_hourly$data_log[[x]],
                       method = m, use = "pairwise.complete.obs"))) %>%
  mutate(var_x = factor(var_x, levels = var_names),
         var_y = factor(var_y, levels = rev(var_names))) 

rm(var_names, cor_order, hc)
```

```{r, fig.width=9, fig.height=9}
p_cor <- corr_long %>%
  ggplot(aes(x = var_x, y = var_y, fill = correlation)) +
  geom_tile(color = "cornsilk3", linewidth = 0.3) +
  geom_text(
    aes(label = ifelse(abs(correlation) >= 0.3, round(correlation, 2), "")),
    size = 3) +
  scale_fill_gradient2(limits = c(-1, 1), midpoint = 0, name = "Correlation") +
  coord_fixed() +
  labs(title = "Correlation matrix",
       subtitle = "Upper triangle: Spearman | Lower triangle: Pearson",
       x = NULL, y = NULL) +
  theme_minimal(base_size = 11) +
  theme(
    axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 45, hjust = 1, vjust = 1),
    axis.text.y = element_text(size = 9),
    panel.grid = element_blank(),
    plot.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
    legend.position = "right")
p_cor
```

```{r}
ggsave("../../outputs/physiology_environment_association/correlations_log.png",
       p_cor, width = 9, height = 9, dpi = 300)
rm(p_cor)
```


## PCA-based reduction of collinear predictors

#### particle

```{r}
particle_large_vars <- c("pc25", "pc5", "pc10")
particle_mass_vars  <- c("pm1", "pm25", "pm10")
particle_small_vars <- c("pc1", "pc03", "pc05")
particle_vars <- c(particle_small_vars, particle_large_vars, particle_mass_vars)

pca_particle_large <- prcomp(log1p(trigger_hourly$data[, particle_large_vars]),
                             center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_particle_mass <- prcomp(log1p(trigger_hourly$data[, particle_mass_vars]),
                            center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_particle_small <- prcomp(log1p(trigger_hourly$data[, particle_small_vars]),
                             center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_particle_all_log <- prcomp(trigger_hourly$data_log[, particle_vars],
                             center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

tibble(
  component = c("particle_large_PC1", "particle_mass_PC1",
                "particle_small_PC1", "particle_all_log_PC1"),
  variables = c(
    paste(particle_large_vars, collapse = ", "),
    paste(particle_mass_vars, collapse = ", "),
    paste(particle_small_vars, collapse = ", "),
    paste(particle_vars, collapse = ", ")),
  pc1_variance = c(
    summary(pca_particle_large)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC1"],
    summary(pca_particle_mass)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC1"],
    summary(pca_particle_small)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC1"],
    summary(pca_particle_all_log)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC1"]),
  pc2_variance = c(
    summary(pca_particle_large)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC2"],
    summary(pca_particle_mass)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC2"],
    summary(pca_particle_small)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC2"],
    summary(pca_particle_all_log)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC2"]),
   pc1_loadings = c(
    paste(round(pca_particle_large$rotation[, 1], 3), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(pca_particle_mass$rotation[, 1], 3), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(pca_particle_small$rotation[, 1], 3), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(pca_particle_all_log$rotation[, 1], 3), collapse = ", ")),
  pc2_loadings = c(
    paste(round(pca_particle_large$rotation[, 2], 3), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(pca_particle_mass$rotation[, 2], 3), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(pca_particle_small$rotation[, 2], 3), collapse = ", "),
    paste(round(pca_particle_all_log$rotation[, 2], 3), collapse = ", ")
  ))
```

#### activity

```{r}
activity_vars <- c("steps", "cal")

pca_activity <- prcomp(trigger_hourly$data[, activity_vars],
                       center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

pca_activity_log <- prcomp(trigger_hourly$data_log[, activity_vars],
                       center = TRUE, scale. = TRUE)

tibble(component = c("activity", "activity_log"),
       variables = rep(paste(activity_vars, collapse = ", "),2),
       pc1_variance = c(
         summary(pca_activity)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC1"],
         summary(pca_activity_log)$importance["Proportion of Variance", "PC1"]),
       pc1_loadings = c(
         paste(round(pca_activity$rotation[, 1], 3), collapse = ", "),
         paste(round(pca_activity_log$rotation[, 1], 3), collapse = ", ")))
```

#### Correlation after pca reduction on log transformed data (when needed)

```{r}
trigger_hourly$data_pca_log <- trigger_hourly$data_log %>%
  select(-all_of(particle_vars)) %>%
  bind_cols(particle = -pca_particle_all_log$x[, 1]) %>%
  relocate(particle) %>%
  select(-all_of(activity_vars)) %>%
  bind_cols(activity = - pca_activity_log$x[, 1]) 

trigger_hourly$data_pca <- trigger_hourly$data %>%
  select(-all_of(particle_vars)) %>%
  bind_cols(particle_small = - pca_particle_small$x[, 1],
            particle_large = - pca_particle_large$x[, 1],
            particle_mass = - pca_particle_mass$x[, 1]) %>%
  relocate(particle_small, particle_large, particle_mass) %>%
  select(-all_of(activity_vars)) %>%
  bind_cols(activity = - pca_activity$x[, 1]) 
```

```{r}
rm(corr_long, activity_vars, list = ls(pattern = "^(particle|pca_)"))
```

## Linear Model Heartrate on log-trasformed data

```{r}
model_data_log <- trigger_hourly$data_pca_log %>%
  as_tibble(rownames = "event") %>%
  left_join(trigger_hourly$meta %>% as_tibble(rownames = "event"),
            by = "event") %>%
  rename(nationality = country) %>%
  mutate(userId = factor(userId, levels = sort(unique(userId))),
         nationality = factor(nationality), 
         hour = factor(hour),
         wday = lubridate::wday(date, label = TRUE, week_start = 1),
         wday = factor(as.character(wday),
                       levels = c("Mon", "Tue", "Wed", "Thu", "Fri", "Sat", "Sun")))
```

```{r}
continuous_predictors_log <- c(
  "particle","temperature", "humidity", "pressure", "sound", "uvb", "light",
  "breathrate", "bphigh", "bplow", "bodytemp", "skintemp", "activity")

model_data_scaled_log <- model_data_log %>%
  mutate(across(all_of(continuous_predictors_log), ~ scale(.x)))
```

```{r}
model_heartrate_global_log <- lmerTest::lmer(
  heartrate ~ 
    particle + activity +
    temperature + humidity + pressure + sound + uvb + light +
    breathrate + bphigh + bplow + bodytemp + skintemp +
    hour + nationality + (1 | userId),
  data = model_data_scaled_log,
  REML = FALSE)
```

```{r}
summary(model_heartrate_global_log)
```

```{r}
performance::check_collinearity(model_heartrate_global_log)
```

```{r}
rm(model_data_scaled_log, model_data_log, continuous_predictors_log)
```

```{r, fig.width=4, fig.height=16}
library(broom.mixed)
library(gt)
library(gtsummary)

gt_coef <- broom.mixed::tidy(model_heartrate_global_log, effects = "fixed") %>%
  filter(
    term != "(Intercept)",
    !stringr::str_detect(term, "^hour")
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    term_label = case_when(
      term == "particle" ~ "Particle PC1 (log1p PM/PC)",
      term == "activity" ~ "Activity PC1 (log1p cal/steps)",
      term == "light" ~ "Light (log1p)",
      term == "uvb" ~ "UVB (log1p)",
      term == "temperature" ~ "Temperature",
      term == "humidity" ~ "Humidity",
      term == "pressure" ~ "Pressure",
      term == "sound" ~ "Sound",
      term == "breathrate" ~ "Breath rate",
      term == "bphigh" ~ "Systolic blood pressure",
      term == "bplow" ~ "Diastolic blood pressure",
      term == "bodytemp" ~ "Body temperature",
      term == "skintemp" ~ "Skin temperature",
      stringr::str_detect(term, "^nationality") ~ 
        stringr::str_replace(term, "^nationality", "Nationality: "),
      TRUE ~ term
    ),
    abs_estimate = abs(estimate),
    p_value = gtsummary::style_pvalue(p.value, digits = 3),
    significance = case_when(
      p.value < 0.001 ~ "***",
      p.value < 0.01  ~ "**",
      p.value < 0.05  ~ "*",
      p.value < 0.1   ~ ".",
      TRUE ~ ""
    )
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(abs_estimate)) %>%
  select(term_label, estimate, std.error, significance) %>%
  gt() %>%
  fmt_number(
    columns = c(estimate, std.error),
    decimals = 3
  ) %>%
  cols_label(
    term_label = "Predictor",
    estimate = html("&beta;"),
    std.error = "SE",
    significance = "Significance"
  ) %>%
  tab_header(
    title = "Fixed effects associated with heart rate",
    subtitle = "Continuous predictors were scaled before model fitting"
  ) %>%
  tab_source_note(
    source_note = "β values refer to one-standard-deviation increase in each continuous predictor. Variables marked as log1p were log-transformed before scaling."
  )

gt_coef
```

```{r}
gt_coef_compact <- gt_coef %>%
  cols_width(
    term_label ~ px(220),
    estimate ~ px(70),
    std.error ~ px(70),
    significance ~ px(80)
  ) %>%
  tab_options(
    table.width = px(460),
    data_row.padding = px(1),
    column_labels.padding = px(2),
    heading.padding = px(3),
    source_notes.padding = px(2),
    table.font.size = px(12)
  )

gtsave(
  gt_coef_compact,
  filename = "../../outputs/physiology_environment_association/heartrate_model_coefficients.png",
  zoom = 3,
  expand = 0
)
```
